2603.16440.title: Capability-Guided Compression: Toward Interpretability-Aware Budget Allocation for Large Language Models
2603.16440.paper_summary: Large language model compression has made substantial progress through pruning, quantization, and low-rank decomposition, yet a fundamental limitation persists across all existing methods: compression budgets are allocated without any representation of what individual model components functionally encode. We term this the capability-blind compression problem and argue it is a root cause of two well-documented failures -- the insensitivity of perplexity-based evaluation to reasoning capability loss, and the abrupt phase transitions in model performance recently characterized by Ma et al. (2026). We propose Capability-Guided Compression (CGC), a framework that addresses this by using Sparse Autoencoder (SAE)-derived capability density maps to allocate differential compression budgets across transformer components. Capability density is a formally defined scalar measure combining the feature breadth, activation entropy, and cross-input consistency of a component's SAE feature activation distribution. We prove theoretically that components with higher capability density exhibit lower structural redundancy and reach their individual phase transition points at lower compression ratios, providing the first pre-compression mechanism for component-level phase transition prediction. Experiments on GPT-2 Medium confirm that capability density is statistically independent of Wanda importance scores (Spearman rho = -0.054, n = 384 heads), establishing it as a genuinely novel compression signal orthogonal to all existing importance metrics. We report a negative result on PPL-based compression comparison and provide a principled diagnosis identifying GPT-2 Medium as an insufficient test bed for the full CGC hypothesis. The theoretical framework, density formalism, and orthogonality finding constitute a foundation for capability-aware compression research.
2603.16440.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型的壓縮在剪枝、量化和低秩分解方面取得了重大進展，但所有現有方法中仍然存在一個根本限制：壓縮預算的分配沒有考慮到各個模型組件功能上編碼的內容。我們將其稱為能力盲壓縮問題，並認為這是兩個已被充分記錄的失敗的根本原因——基於困惑度的評估對推理能力損失的不敏感性，以及最近由 Ma 等人（2026）描述的模型性能的突變相變。 我們提出了能力引導壓縮（CGC），這是一個通過使用稀疏自編碼器（SAE）衍生的能力密度圖來在Transformer組件之間分配差異壓縮預算的框架。能力密度是一個正式定義的標量度量，結合了組件的特徵廣度、激活熵和跨輸入一致性。 我們在理論上證明，具有較高能力密度的組件表現出較低的結構冗餘，並在較低的壓縮比下達到各自的相變點，提供了組件級相變預測的首個預壓縮機制。對 GPT-2 Medium 的實驗確認能力密度與 Wanda 重要性分數在統計上是獨立的（Spearman rho = -0.054，n = 384 heads），確立了它作為一種真正新穎的壓縮信號，與所有現有的重要性度量正交。我們報告了基於 PPL 的壓縮比較的負面結果，並提供了一個原則性的診斷，確定 GPT-2 Medium 作為 CGC 假設的完整測試床是不足夠的。這一理論框架、密度形式主義和正交性發現構成了能力感知壓縮研究的基礎。
2603.16440.author: Rishaank Gupta et.al.
